noveltyJustification: This iteration adds ticket-ready scopes, dependencies, verification gates, and merge/defer decisions without new source discovery.
summary: Drafted ten remediation packets ordered by correctness prerequisites, host lifecycle cleanup, and lower-priority resident-memory/read-path work.

scope: Track in-flight index tasks per project_root, cancel and bounded-await active indexing before remove_project pops registry entries or calls Project.close, and prevent close-under-write.
acceptanceCriteria: remove_project detects active _run_index
task cancel is idempotent and bounded
registry entries are popped only after active work settles
stale load-time waiters are released
tests cover remove-before-task-start, remove-after-project-capture, readd race, and close-under-write
verification: cd .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q -k "remove_project or cancel or index"

scope: Add protocol, client, daemon dispatch, and timeout/disconnect behavior for project/request-scoped index cancellation.
acceptanceCriteria: CancelIndexRequest and response are encoded in protocol union
DaemonClient exposes a cancel method independent of closing the connection
daemon dispatch routes cancel to tracked in-flight task
server refresh/search timeout paths attempt cancel
repeated cancel is safe
verification: cd .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q -k "cancel_index or protocol or timeout"
nonGoals: remove_project race fix beyond consuming packet 1 primitive

scope: Replace fire-and-forget daemon/MCP background index tasks with tracked ownership, cancellation, done callbacks, and observable exceptions.
acceptanceCriteria: daemon load-time _run_index tasks are tracked
inner index tasks are included in shutdown await/cancel
CLI and server _bg_index tasks have owners
executor cancellation calls daemon cancel where available
background exceptions are logged
verification: cd .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q -k "bg_index or background or shutdown"

dependency: independent; should precede broad process sweep
scope: Enforce one code-graph launcher/server owner per effective DB dir, bridge to active owner, reap stale PPID 1 children safely, and call closeDb on shutdown.
acceptanceCriteria: lease includes DB-dir identity
duplicate launcher bridges or reports owner
stale owner reclaim is liveness and DB-dir checked
signal path reaps child server
shutdown calls closeDb
orphan reap supports dry-run

dependency: after or coordinated with bg-index and code-graph lifecycle packets
scope: Add dry-run-first inventory and stale-process cleanup for MCP child processes and lingering CLI executor processes across deep-flow sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: inventory classifies ccc mcp, ccc run-daemon, sidecar, code-graph launcher/server, and CLI executor processes
parent PID, elapsed time, workspace/spec lineage, and safety state are reported
dry-run is default
current session is protected
termination requires allowlist or age/lineage proof
verification: current PID and parent-chain safety tests
manual dry-run inventory before any termination command
nonGoals: claiming multiple ccc mcp children are one daemon leak

scope: Give rerank sidecar explicit PID/port metadata, healthy reuse, stale PID detection, parent/session exit cleanup, bounded startup, and non-destructive stop/reap behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: start writes metadata
ensure reuses healthy sidecar
failed health startup terminates child and closes log handles
stale sidecars are detectable and stoppable
MCP startup does not spawn duplicate sidecars for same port
verification: sidecar start/health/stop integration test on a temporary port
nonGoals: P0 leak-growth claim without successful runtime measurement

scope: Make Project.close idempotent and deeper: release SQLite, clear strong App/Environment/context references where safe, and document upstream resources that remain process-owned.
acceptanceCriteria: Project.close can be called repeatedly
SQLite handle closes exactly once
_app/_env references are cleared or made inert after close
close cannot interleave with active index/update
tests prove no DB use after close and observable object release where possible
verification: cd .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q -k "Project.close or close_project or remove_project"
nonGoals: embedder hash cleanup except direct Project ownership

scope: Fix ProjectRegistry embedder/config-hash cleanup asymmetry and config-refresh stale embedder retention.
acceptanceCriteria: changed config evicts or reference-counts old embedder hashes
remove_project and close_all handle embedder cache intentionally
same-hash, changed-hash, remove, and shutdown paths are tested
tests distinguish true embedder retention from self-healing dict overwrites
verification: cd .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q -k "embedder or config_hash or refresh_project"
nonGoals: LiteLLM upstream global shutdown unless represented by embedder abstraction

dependency: after correctness and host lifecycle packets unless immediate RSS relief is required
scope: Add close/unload lifecycle to reranker adapters and adjacent model/client adapters, close httpx pools, clear model refs, expose close_all_adapters, and define LiteLLM handling.
acceptanceCriteria: CrossEncoder, HttpSidecar, and Jina adapters expose idempotent close/unload
HttpSidecar closes _client and _fallback_adapter
_ADAPTERS clear path calls close before dropping refs
daemon shutdown invokes adapter cleanup
tests cover sidecar key, bare key, fallback key, and env toggles
verification: cd .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests -q -k "rerank or adapter or sidecar"
nonGoals: P0 severity without successful-search RSS growth

scope: Reduce repeated short-lived git children and passive socket/transport retention on code-graph read paths.
acceptanceCriteria: git HEAD/diff freshness checks are cached or batched with TTL
git errors fall back safely
active socket/transport maps have idle cleanup or heartbeat
metrics/logs expose cache hits and active transport count
stale-index detection is not regressed

summary: Merge adapter findings into one P2 adapter lifecycle packet, subsume F-021 under protocol cancel, group F-006/F-013 with F-027, keep remove-project safety separate and first because it supplies the daemon-internal primitive for protocol cancel.

The smallest safe first implementation packet is remove-project-cancel-safety because it is source-local, does not require public protocol design, and blocks unsafe close/remove races before any cancellation API is exposed.

Downgrade the initial P0 memory-first framing for F-001/F-002/F-003; keep sidecar lifecycle P1/P2 and adapter lifecycle P2 until successful search or fallback measurement shows growth rather than process inventory.

hypothesis: Protocol cancel and remove-project safety should be one packet
reason: The daemon-internal race in remove_project exists without a public cancel verb and must be fixed before protocol cancel has a safe primitive to call.

hypothesis: Multiple ccc mcp processes prove an in-daemon leak
reason: Iteration 007 observed parent spread and stable counts compatible with multiple MCP host/session registrations; treat as host/session lifecycle sweep, not daemon duplication.

hypothesis: Adapter lifecycle should keep P0 priority based on current measurement
reason: Iteration 006 could not capture daemon RSS and iteration 007 measured process inventory plus flat VM counters during failed commands, not successful-search or fallback growth.
